- More saturated/darker background colors
- Darker text colors

Done! I've updated the pill colors in both files to be more pronounced in light mode while keeping the dark mode colors unchanged:

**Light mode (new):**
- **Done/Completed**: Green background (`bg-green-100`) with dark green text (`text-green-700`)
- **Running/In Progress**: Yellow background (`bg-yellow-100`) with dark yellow text (`text-yellow-700`)
- **Failed/Error**: Red background (`bg-red-100`) with dark red text (`text-red-700`)
- **Pending/Queued**: Gray background (`bg-gray-200`) with dark gray text (`text-gray-700`)
- **Chat type**: Purple background (`bg-purple-100`) with dark purple text (`text-purple-700`)
- **Audio/Voice type**: Orange background (`bg-orange-100`) with dark orange text (`text-orange-700`)

**Dark mode (unchanged):**
- All colors remain the same with the semi-transparent backgrounds (e.g., `bg-green-500/20 text-green-400`)

```diff
@@ -497,4 +497,37 @@
  // Danger/Delete button
  className = "text-muted-foreground hover:text-red-500 hover:bg-red-500/10";
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ ### Status/Type Badge (Pill) Styles
+ 
+ Status and type badges use theme-aware colors with more pronounced light mode colors and subtle dark mode colors:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ // Status badges - pattern: bg-{color}-100 text-{color}-700 dark:bg-{color}-500/20 dark:text-{color}-400
+ // Success (done, completed)
+ className = "bg-green-100 text-green-700 dark:bg-green-500/20 dark:text-green-400";
+ 
+ // Warning (running, in_progress)
+ className = "bg-yellow-100 text-yellow-700 dark:bg-yellow-500/20 dark:text-yellow-400";
+ 
+ // Error (failed, error)
+ className = "bg-red-100 text-red-700 dark:bg-red-500/20 dark:text-red-400";
+ 
+ // Neutral (pending, queued, default)
+ className = "bg-gray-200 text-gray-700 dark:bg-gray-500/20 dark:text-gray-400";
+ 
+ // Type badges
+ // Chat type
+ className = "bg-purple-100 text-purple-700 dark:bg-purple-500/20 dark:text-purple-400";
+ 
+ // Audio/Voice type
+ className = "bg-orange-100 text-orange-700 dark:bg-orange-500/20 dark:text-orange-400";
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ **Badge base structure:**
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ &lt;span className={&#96;inline-flex items-center px-2.5 py-1 rounded-md text-xs font-medium ${badgeClass}&#96;}&gt;
+   {label}
+ &lt;/span&gt;
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

```diff
  ### Styling
  
- - **Never use hardcoded colors** like &#96;bg-black&#96;, &#96;bg-[#1a1a1a]&#96;, &#96;text-white&#96;, &#96;border-[#333]&#96; - these break light mode
- - **Always use CSS variable classes**: &#96;bg-background&#96;, &#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;border-border&#96;, &#96;bg-muted&#96;, &#96;bg-popover&#96;
+ - **Never use hardcoded colors** like &#96;bg-black&#96;, &#96;bg-[#1a1a1a]&#96;, &#96;text-white&#96;, &#96;border-[#333]&#96;, &#96;text-gray-300&#96;, &#96;text-gray-400&#96; - these break light mode
+ - **Always use CSS variable classes**: &#96;bg-background&#96;, &#96;text-foreground&#96;, &#96;border-border&#96;, &#96;bg-muted&#96;, &#96;text-muted-foreground&#96;, &#96;bg-accent&#96;, &#96;bg-popover&#96;
  - **Checkboxes need visible borders**: Use &#96;border-muted-foreground&#96; (not &#96;border-border&#96;) and &#96;border-2&#96; for custom checkbox buttons to ensure visibility in both light and dark modes
  
```

</details>

</tool-use>

1. **Added `headerActions` prop to `AppLayout`** - A new optional prop that renders content beside the user profile dropdown in the header.

```diff
@@ -391,10 +389,34 @@
  
  **AppLayout Props:**
- 
  - &#96;activeItem&#96;: Current nav item ID for highlighting
  - &#96;onItemChange&#96;: Callback when nav item clicked
  - &#96;sidebarOpen&#96; / &#96;onSidebarToggle&#96;: Sidebar collapse state
- - &#96;customHeader&#96;: Optional React node for custom header content (left side)
- - &#96;headerActions&#96;: Optional React node for action buttons beside user profile (right side)
+ - &#96;customHeader&#96;: Optional React node for custom header content (left side of header bar)
+ - &#96;headerActions&#96;: Optional React node for action buttons beside user profile dropdown (right side of header bar)
+ 
+ ### Detail Page Pattern
+ 
+ Detail pages (AgentDetail, SimulationDetail) place their navigation and action buttons **in the content area**, not in the header. This provides a consistent layout:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ // Inside AppLayout children, at the top of the content
+ &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between -mt-4"&gt;
+   &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-3"&gt;
+     &lt;button onClick={() =&gt; router.push("/list-page")} className="..."&gt;
+       {/* Back arrow icon */}
+     &lt;/button&gt;
+     &lt;h1 className="text-xl font-semibold"&gt;{item.name}&lt;/h1&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+   &lt;button className="h-9 px-6 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-foreground text-background ..."&gt;
+     Save / Launch / Action
+   &lt;/button&gt;
+ &lt;/div&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Key details:
+ - Use &#96;-mt-4&#96; to pull the header row up closer to the top
+ - Back button + title on the left, action button on the right
+ - Title uses &#96;text-xl font-semibold&#96;
+ - Action buttons use &#96;h-9 px-6 text-sm&#96; sizing
  
  ### Component Patterns
```

</details>

```diff
@@ -396,29 +396,54 @@
  - &#96;headerActions&#96;: Optional React node for action buttons beside user profile dropdown (right side of header bar)
  
- ### Detail Page Pattern
+ ### Detail Page Patterns
  
- Detail pages (AgentDetail, SimulationDetail) place their navigation and action buttons **in the content area**, not in the header. This provides a consistent layout:
+ There are two patterns for detail pages:
+ 
+ **1. Content Area Pattern (AgentDetail)**
+ Places navigation and action buttons in the content area:
  
  &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
  // Inside AppLayout children, at the top of the content
  &lt;div className="flex items-center justify-between -mt-4"&gt;
    &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-3"&gt;
-     &lt;button onClick={() =&gt; router.push("/list-page")} className="..."&gt;
-       {/* Back arrow icon */}
+     &lt;button onClick={() =&gt; router.push("/list-page")} className="w-8 h-8 ..."&gt;
+       {/* Back arrow icon w-5 h-5 */}
      &lt;/button&gt;
      &lt;h1 className="text-xl font-semibold"&gt;{item.name}&lt;/h1&gt;
    &lt;/div&gt;
    &lt;button className="h-9 px-6 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-foreground text-background ..."&gt;
-     Save / Launch / Action
+     Save
    &lt;/button&gt;
  &lt;/div&gt;
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
  
- Key details:
- 
- - Use &#96;-mt-4&#96; to pull the header row up closer to the top
- - Back button + title on the left, action button on the right
- - Title uses &#96;text-xl font-semibold&#96;
- - Action buttons use &#96;h-9 px-6 text-sm&#96; sizing
+ **2. Header Pattern (SimulationDetail)**
+ Places navigation in the header bar using &#96;customHeader&#96; and &#96;headerActions&#96;:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ const customHeader = (
+   &lt;div className="flex items-center gap-3"&gt;
+     &lt;button className="w-8 h-8 rounded-md hover:bg-muted ..."&gt;
+       {/* Back arrow icon w-5 h-5 */}
+     &lt;/button&gt;
+     &lt;span className="text-base font-semibold"&gt;{item.name}&lt;/span&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+ );
+ 
+ const headerActions = (
+   &lt;div className="relative mr-2"&gt;  {/* mr-2 adds spacing from profile dropdown */}
+     &lt;button className="h-8 px-4 rounded-md text-sm font-medium bg-foreground text-background ..."&gt;
+       Launch
+     &lt;/button&gt;
+   &lt;/div&gt;
+ );
+ 
+ &lt;AppLayout customHeader={customHeader} headerActions={headerActions}&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Key differences:
+ - Content area pattern uses larger title (&#96;text-xl&#96;) and larger action button (&#96;h-9 px-6&#96;)
+ - Header pattern uses smaller title (&#96;text-base&#96;) and compact action button (&#96;h-8 px-4&#96;)
+ - Header pattern adds &#96;mr-2&#96; to action wrapper for spacing from user profile dropdown
  
  ### Component Patterns
```

</details>

</tool-use>

Done! I've moved the agent name and save button to the header. Here's what was changed:

**AgentDetail.tsx:**
- Added `AgentDetailHeaderState` type export
- Added `onHeaderStateChange` optional prop that receives header state (name, loading, saving, callbacks)
- When `onHeaderStateChange` is provided, the internal header is hidden
- Uses `useEffect` to notify parent when header-relevant state changes

**agents/[uuid]/page.tsx:**
- Imports `AgentDetailHeaderState` from AgentDetail
- Manages header state via callback from AgentDetail
- Renders back button + agent name in `customHeader`
- Renders save button in `headerActions` with `mr-2` spacing from profile dropdown
- Styling matches the simulation page (back button `w-8 h-8`, name `text-base font-semibold`, save button `h-8 px-4`)

```diff
- ### Detail Page Patterns
- 
- There are two patterns for detail pages:
- 
- **1. Content Area Pattern (AgentDetail)**
- Places navigation and action buttons in the content area:
- 
- &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
- // Inside AppLayout children, at the top of the content
```

```diff
@@ -442,9 +421,45 @@
  &#96;&#96;&#96;
  
- Key differences:
- 
- - Content area pattern uses larger title (&#96;text-xl&#96;) and larger action button (&#96;h-9 px-6&#96;)
- - Header pattern uses smaller title (&#96;text-base&#96;) and compact action button (&#96;h-8 px-4&#96;)
- - Header pattern adds &#96;mr-2&#96; to action wrapper for spacing from user profile dropdown
+ **Callback Pattern for Component-based Detail Pages:**
+ 
+ When the detail content is a separate component (like &#96;AgentDetail&#96;), use a callback to lift header state to the page:
+ 
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;tsx
+ // In component (AgentDetail.tsx)
+ export type AgentDetailHeaderState = {
+   agentName: string;
+   isLoading: boolean;
+   isSaving: boolean;
+   onSave: () =&gt; void;
+   onEditName: () =&gt; void;
+ };
+ 
+ // Component accepts callback and hides internal header when provided
+ type Props = {
+   agentUuid: string;
+   onHeaderStateChange?: (state: AgentDetailHeaderState) =&gt; void;
+ };
+ 
+ // useEffect notifies parent when state changes
+ useEffect(() =&gt; {
+   if (onHeaderStateChange) {
+     onHeaderStateChange({ agentName, isLoading, isSaving, onSave, onEditName });
+   }
+ }, [agentName, isLoading, isSaving, onHeaderStateChange]);
+ 
+ // In page - use callback to build header
+ const [headerState, setHeaderState] = useState&lt;AgentDetailHeaderState | null&gt;(null);
+ const handleHeaderStateChange = useCallback((state) =&gt; setHeaderState(state), []);
+ 
+ &lt;AppLayout customHeader={...} headerActions={...}&gt;
+   &lt;AgentDetail agentUuid={uuid} onHeaderStateChange={handleHeaderStateChange} /&gt;
+ &lt;/AppLayout&gt;
+ &#96;&#96;&#96;
+ 
+ Key styling:
+ - Back button: &#96;w-8 h-8&#96;, icon &#96;w-5 h-5&#96;
+ - Title: &#96;text-base font-semibold&#96;
+ - Action button: &#96;h-8 px-4 text-sm&#96;
+ - Action wrapper: &#96;mr-2&#96; for spacing from profile dropdown
  
  ### Component Patterns
```

---

Updated `.cursor/rules/app-details.md` with:
- Renamed section to "Detail Page Header Pattern" (both AgentDetail and SimulationDetail now use the same approach)
- Removed obsolete "Content Area Pattern" 
- Added documentation for the **callback pattern** used when detail content is a separate component:
  - Export a header state type from the component
  - Accept `onHeaderStateChange` callback prop
  - Use `useEffect` to notify parent when state changes
  - Component hides internal header when callback is provided
- Included code examples for both the component and page sides of this pattern
